_comment: Subset read-only door for the student-union consumer. This policy is meant for a
SECOND gateway process (deploy/systemd/user/rtime-library-gateway-public.service,
HTTP 127.0.0.1:8781) — isolation is process-level: independent process + this
policy file + its own port. It does NOT rely on the self-reported MCP client name
(unauthenticated); 'clients' below defines ONLY 'default', so every caller of this
process gets the same restrictive rule no matter what name it reports.
allowed_path_prefixes confines EVERY read to these brain subtrees (gate-enforced:
path args must resolve inside them; search/recent/tree/list get the prefix
injected or must pass one; other reads without a path are denied).
>>> CONFIRM THE REAL PREFIXES AT DEPLOY TIME: verified 2026-07-02 against the
>>> live brain (lib.search): activity content lives under
>>> knowledge/institutions/ustc/activities/ — there is NO knowledge/activities
>>> top-level dir, so the single ustc prefix below covers everything public.
>>> Re-verify with lib.tree on the owner instance before enabling the unit.
Writes are denied by THREE independent locks: default_write=deny (tier switch),
the client deny globs below (lib.settings.*, lib.contribute, lib.finalize,
lib.course-intake, lib.jobs.*), and the allow list (non-empty => anything not
listed is denied, including every write and lib.jobs.get/list reads).
'methods' is intentionally OMITTED: tiers then come from METHOD_TIERS in gate.py,
so this file can never drift behind newly added methods (a new method would be
denied by the allow list until explicitly added here).
audit_log is separate from the owner instance's audit.jsonl so the two processes
never interleave their audit streams.
